返回本批次

Sens 要求加巴德告诉美国人，VPN 使用会使他们受到监视

Sens Ask Gabbard to Tell Americans That VPN Use Subjects Them to Surveillance

www.techdirt.com 作者 c420 分数 36 评论 9 2026/04/05 07:27
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 来自无趣的老故事改编部
  2. 这可能不是真正的“怀登海妖”，但它仍然附有他的名字。这里所说的并不像他今年早些时候发给中央情报局领导层的这句话那么不祥：
  3. 我写这封信是为了提醒您注意今天早些时候我发给您的一封机密信，我在信中对中央情报局的活动表示深切关注。
  4. 很少有人能够用如此少的内容说出这么多。这篇文章的篇幅稍长一些，但它的含义可能比怀登和其他人在最近写给特朗普（讽刺已经死了）国家情报总监图尔西·加巴德的一封信中提出的表面问题更深层次。以下是戴尔卡梅伦为《连线》报道的详细信息：
  5. 在周四发给国家情报总监图尔西·加巴德 (Tulsi Gabbard) 的一封信中，立法者表示，由于 VPN 掩盖了用户的真实位置，而且情报机构认为来源不明的通信是外国的，美国人可能会无意中放弃他们根据法律有权获得的隐私保护。包括联邦调查局、国家安全局和联邦贸易委员会在内的多个联邦机构都建议消费者使用 VPN 来保护自己的隐私。但遵循这一建议可能会无意中让美国人失去他们正在寻求的保护。这封信由民主党进步派成员签署：参议员罗恩·怀登、伊丽莎白·沃伦、爱德华·马基和亚历克斯·帕迪拉，以及众议员普拉米拉·贾亚帕尔和萨拉·雅各布斯。

Original Summary

  1. from the unfun-twist-on-an-old-story dept
  2. This may not be an actual “Wyden siren,” but it still has his name attached to it. What’s being said here isn’t nearly as ominous as this single sentence he sent to CIA leadership earlier this year:
  3. I write to alert you to a classified letter I sent you earlier today in which I express deep concerns about CIA activities.
  4. Few people are capable of saying so much with so little. This one runs a bit longer, but it has implications that likely run deeper than the surface level issue raised by Wyden and others in a recent letter to Trump’s (satire is dead) Director of National Intelligence, Tulsi Gabbard. Here are the details, as reported by Dell Cameron for Wired:
  5. In a letter sent Thursday to Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, the lawmakers say that because VPNs obscure a user’s true location, and because intelligence agencies presume that communications of unknown origin are foreign, Americans may be inadvertently waiving the privacy protections they’re entitled to under the law. Several federal agencies, including the FBI, the National Security Agency, and the Federal Trade Commission, have recommended that consumers use VPNs to protect their privacy. But following that advice may inadvertently cost Americans the very protections they’re seeking. The letter was signed by members of the Democratic Party’s progressive flank: Senators Ron Wyden, Elizabeth Warren, Edward Markey, and Alex Padilla, along with Representatives Pramila Jayapal and Sara Jacobs.

评论（默认中文）

everdrive 2026/04/05 09:19
几乎可以肯定，这并不像听起来那么有趣。这实际上是说，如果您在美国，您的许多通信的网络路径可能是从美国的一个地区到另一个地区。 （例如：犹他州到帕洛阿尔托，纽约到弗吉尼亚州等）这些通信不会受到监视。

但是，如果您 100% 通过消费者 VPN 发送通信，因此您的大部分网络流量源自美国境外，那么您的流量最终可能会被自动收集。

This is almost certainly much less interesting than it sounds. It's effectively saying that if you're in the US, your network path is probably from one part of the US to another for many of your communications. (eg: Utah to Palo Alto, New York to Virginia, etc) These communications would not be surveilled.

But, if you send 100% of your communications through a consumer VPN and therefore much of your network traffic originates outside the US, your traffic might end up getting automatically collected.

在 HN 中查看
ra 2026/04/05 10:18
换句话说，如果您在美国境外，您的 VPN 流量很容易被拦截和存储。

如果您在美国境内并使用 VPN，他们会假设您来自美国境外：拦截和存储。

In other words, if you're outside of the US your VPN traffic is fair game for interception and storage.

If you're inside the US and use a VPN, they'll assume you're from outside of the US: Intercep and store.

在 HN 中查看
mmh0000 2026/04/05 10:48
公平地说……这已经众所周知了很长时间：

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PRISM

To be fair… this has been known for a long time:

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PRISM

在 HN 中查看
halJordan 2026/04/05 11:22
不，如果您在美国境外并且是美国公民，那么您的流量就不是公平的游戏（尽管如果不是立即显而易见的话，他们确实会相当自由地假设）。

如果您的流量源自或终止于美国，那么这是不公平的游戏

关键是 VPN 混淆了你的来源。这就是VPN的意义所在，所以表现得惊讶有点傻。

No, if you're outside the US and a us citizen, your traffic is not fair game (although they do assume pretty freely if it's not immediately obvious).

If your traffic originates or terminates in the us it is not fair game

The point is that the vpn obfuscates your origin. Which is the point of a vpn so acting surprised is a little silly.

gnabgib 2026/04/05 07:29

Related: Using a VPN May Subject You to NSA Spying (12 points) https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47548968

givemeethekeys 2026/04/05 08:35
不使用 VPN 是否可以让您免受监视？
Does not using a VPN free you from surveillance?
在 HN 中查看
MPSimmons 2026/04/05 08:44
理论上 政府对监控美国公民有_一些_限制（https://www.brennancenter.org/our-work/research-reports/soci...），而且根据原始文章，如果您使用 VPN，这些听起来都不适用。
THEORETICALLY the government has _some_ restrictions against monitoring US citizens (https://www.brennancenter.org/our-work/research-reports/soci...), and it sounds like none of those are deemed to apply if you're using a VPN, per the original article.
在 HN 中查看
jeffbee 2026/04/05 09:17
许多VPN由有组织的犯罪组织、各国情报部门和警察拥有和运营。 VPN 以某种方式保护你的想法并不具有理性基础。
Many VPNs are owned and operated by organized crime, intelligence services from all kinds of nations, and cops. The idea that a VPN somehow protects you does not enjoy a rational basis.

OutOfHere 2026/04/05 09:20
别无选择，只能使用外国 VPN 来访问某些网站，特别是某些加密货币和预测市场网站，否则这些网站将无法访问。
There is no choice but to use a foreign VPN to access certain sites, specifically certain crypto and prediction market sites that otherwise remain inaccessible.
